title: Kefta skewers
subtitle: Moroccan spiced minced meat skewers
excerpt: Minced beef or lamb mixed with onion, cumin, paprika, coriander, and fresh herbs, then moulded around skewers and grilled over high heat until charred and juicy. The simplest Moroccan street food. Served with flatbread, harissa, and a squeeze of lemon.
sourceNotes: Kefta grilled on skewers is found across the Maghreb and the broader Middle East and Central Asia under various names (kafta, kofte, keftes). The Moroccan version uses cumin, paprika, and a high proportion of fresh coriander and parsley. It is sold from street grills in every Moroccan city, particularly in the Djemaa el Fna square in Marrakech.

recipe.freezeNotes: Shape the kefta around skewers and freeze on a tray before transferring to bags. Cook from frozen, adding 3 to 4 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: The raw mixture keeps refrigerated for 1 day.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The mixture can be made and shaped 24 hours ahead, then refrigerated.

content.text: The mixture must be worked vigorously by hand to develop the proteins — this is what allows the kefta to hold its shape on the skewer without falling. Mix for at least 2 minutes until the mixture feels sticky and cohesive. A 15% fat mince is ideal; leaner mince produces drier kefta.

Flatten metal skewers (broad and flat, not round) grip the mixture better than round ones. If using round metal or wooden skewers, make the kefta logs slightly thicker and shorter. Refrigerate for 30 minutes after shaping — chilling firms the fat and helps them hold on the grill.

Combine the {{beef-mince}}, grated {{onion}}, {{garlic}}, {{cumin-ground}}, {{paprika-sweet}}, {{coriander-ground}}, {{cinnamon-ground}}, {{coriander}}, {{parsley-flat}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}} in a bowl. Mix with your hands for 2 minutes, kneading and squeezing until the mixture is completely uniform and feels sticky. Divide into 8 equal portions. Mould each portion around a skewer in a sausage shape about 15 cm long, pressing firmly. Refrigerate for 30 minutes. Heat a grill or griddle pan to the highest possible setting. Grill the kefta for 3 to 4 minutes per side until well charred on the outside and just cooked through. Serve with flatbread, harissa, and lemon wedges.

items.cause: Mixture not worked enough or too lean, or not chilled before grilling
items.fix: Mix more vigorously until the mixture feels almost like a paste. Chill for at least 30 minutes. If they still fall, shape them into patties instead and grill flat
Use mince with at least 15% fat. Do not cook beyond 4 minutes per side or the fat will be cooked out
The grill or pan must be at maximum heat before the kefta go on. A low heat steams the meat rather than charring it

content.text: Kefta grilled on street braziers is one of the defining smells of Moroccan medinas. In Marrakech's Djemaa el Fna, rows of kefta stalls light up at dusk and the smoke from the charcoal grills drifts across the square. This is also a home dish — a quick weeknight supper made in under 30 minutes. The same mixture can be shaped into patties for a burger-style preparation, or rolled into small balls for kefta tagine cooked in tomato sauce with eggs.
